violations.text: The door(screen) to the outside, located in the (back kitchen ), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/04/2025

Wood is used in the construction of (ceiling support) in the (walk in cooler ) area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/29/2025

The condensate from the ( walk in cooler) refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
Outside refuse area observed with boxes, pallets, and /or other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

Raw animal foods (shelled eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (box of limes and bagged vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of black and pink residue. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service to be washed and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

(Chicharron and beef), which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours.PIC voluntarily dispsosed of food product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

( Cooked meats and prepared salads) was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per interview of (PIC ), (stewed chicken ) was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep.PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
Various food product observed being stored in non food grade plastic bags. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses.PIC informed of proper utensil storage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

(Prep unit) located in the (front counter) area was not in good repair.Ambient temperatures observed at 51DF. Establishment not allowed store any TCS foods in unit. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
Inspector observed a leak under 3 bay sink.Systems must b e maintained in goo drepair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025
Outside refuse area observed with an accumulation of boxes, litter, and/or debris.Refuse areas must be kept clean as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/12/2025

The (screen door ) in the (kitchen ) area is in poor repair. Observed screen door detached and warped from door surface. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 08/19/2025
Junctures at 3 bay sink where basins join were observed in poor repair, affecting its ability to be properly cleaned and sanitized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/15/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination in the walk-in cooler. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 25 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer was adjusted to the correct concentration during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024

Stew and Chicken were observed held at (92 ad 70) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods were reheated during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024

Hotel pans of food were observed stacked in the chest freezer and walk-in cooler. Foods were observed in direct contact with bottom of hotel pans. PIC informed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2024
Junctures at 3 bay sink where basins join were observed in poor repair, affecting its ability to be properly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2024
Non-food grade bins were observed being used for marinating chicken. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2024
3 bay sink was observed leaking unto the floor. 3 bay sink faucet also observed leaking. Plumbing systems to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2024
The floor tiles under 3 bay sink were observed in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2024
Walk-in cooler light was observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/13/2024
